generic_rules: Place assumptions and guarantees in the component type scope when they refer to component ports.
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes.
Use concrete bounds, named constants, or visible relations when defining range assumptions and guarantees.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are defined within `annex agree { ... };` blocks.
Use `eq` to define boolean helper variables for range checks (e.g., `eq range_value : bool = x >= min_value and x <= max_value;`).
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes.
Avoid encoding AADL properties (e.g., Compute_Execution_Time) directly as AGREE expressions unless they are exposed as typed ports or constants.
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers for range comparisons.
